explicit_text: When we moved to West Asheville in 1928, it was quite a change from where we had lived before. We started out in an apartment on Covington Street, then rented a house on Langwell, and finally bought a house on Majestic in July 1930. I became very involved in the community, especially through the PTA at the local schools like Aycock and Vance Elementary. The PTA was a big part of my life, helping with lunchrooms, fundraising, and supporting children during the Depression years.

explicit_text: I grew up in Indiana, which was quite different from the mountainous area of Asheville. The rolling hills around our home there were gentle compared to the mountains here. Moving from Indiana to North Carolina was a big change for me and my family.

explicit_text: My husband worked for the State Highway Commission when we first moved to Asheville. He was initially assigned to Marshall, where he experienced the floods shortly after we arrived in 1928. Later, as the highway funds diminished, he realized the program was winding down and started looking for other work. The Highway Commission was a significant part of our early years in North Carolina.

explicit_text: When we first came to Asheville, my husband was assigned to Marshall in 1928. Shortly after we arrived, there was a flood that forced us to evacuate quickly with our young son. We had to stay on a side hill behind the Madison County Courthouse for several days before finding an apartment. It was a tough introduction to the area but memorable.

explicit_text: During the flood in Marshall in 1928, we were carried up the side hill behind the Madison County Courthouse to safety.
